design_philosophy.core_loop: Follow instructor motion sequences by stepping on Power Pad buttons in time with music; accumulate score through accurate pattern matching
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Maintaining accuracy against an active penalty system that decreases score with each mistake, racing against a failing threshold
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Participate in aerobics instruction and musical expression through physical movement with a game controller
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Treats exercise and rhythm gameplay as equivalent entertainment; uses a penalty-based scoring system that can result in failure; integrates freeform creative expression (Tune Up mode) alongside rigid pattern memorization

difficulty.continues_system: Aerobics Mode allows continued play after score reaches zero; Normal Mode ends on failure
difficulty.checkpointing: Progression unlocks new songs and difficulty levels; earlier songs remain available for replay
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players choose which aerobics class/song to attempt; can replay earlier content; Aerobics Mode removes failure condition entirely, allowing self-paced practice

progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing aerobics classes unlocks additional harder songs (8 total: 4 initial + 4 unlockable)
replayability_hooks: Pad Antics Mode offers three distinct sub-games unrelated to story progression (Tune Up, Mat Melodies, Ditto)
Freeform musical composition in Tune Up mode
Aerobics Mode removes failure stakes for practice-oriented replay
Multiple difficulty tiers within Normal Mode progression

world_building_techniques: Aerobics studio setting with instructor character present in gameplay
Themed song titles (Studio 5, Studio 10, Studio 15, Studio 20) suggest progression through increasingly advanced classes

environmental_storytelling: Aerobics studio framing positions player as student following professional instruction
Progressive difficulty naming convention implies structured fitness curriculum
